title: Fertility and financial risk-taking
summary:
We examine how fertility expectations influence financial risk-taking using nationally representative data from three countries. Our results indicate that childless adults who do not expect children are 21-36% more likely to invest in stocks than those who expect children, controlling for personal characteristics. This effect persists also when medical infertility instruments expectations. We find no […]

title: The economics of unions
summary:
My best read of the evidence is that a union raises wages by around 7% for currently unionized employees. The wage gains from a redistribution of rents evenly across workers. Wage compression exists, but redistribution from worker to worker is only a small part. These are the current effects – unionizing more of the economy […]

title: Robin (it’s happening)
summary:
Scientific discovery is driven by the iterative process of observation, hypothesis generation, experimentation, and data analysis. Despite recent advancements in applying artificial intelligence to biology, no system has yet automated all these stages [1, 2, 3]. Here, we introduce Robin, the first multi-agent system capable of fully automating both hypothesis generation and data analysis for […]

title: Conscious introspection leads to more self-deception?
summary:
It seems, then, that we need another signal that can add precision to our introspection. An internal monologue is the experience of having concrete, “narration-style” thoughts as opposed to passive experiences. This argument maybe […]
